body:
While working on link-foundation/command-stream#160, I compared the Rust template release scripts and found a release-loop risk in `scripts/version-and-commit.rs`.

## Problem

`collect_changelog()` reads every `changelog.d/*.md` fragment and prepends the combined text into `CHANGELOG.md`, but it does not remove the consumed fragment files. The release commit stages `Cargo.toml`, optional `Cargo.lock`, and `CHANGELOG.md`, leaving the source fragments in the repository.

Because `scripts/get-bump-type.rs` treats any non-README fragment as `has_fragments=true`, the next push to `main` can decide another release is needed from the same already-consumed fragments.

## Expected behavior

After fragments are collected into `CHANGELOG.md`, the release script should remove the consumed fragment files and stage `changelog.d/` with `git add -A` so the release commit clears them.

## Reference fix

In command-stream PR 161, I patched the copied Rust release script to:

- call `fs::remove_file()` for each collected fragment after writing `CHANGELOG.md`
- run `git add -A <changelog.d>` along with the manifest/changelog staging

This should be applied back to the template to avoid propagating the release-loop risk to new Rust repos.
title: Release script should remove consumed changelog fragments
